title: AP & Payroll for OSP Engineering
subhead: Managing offshore draftsmen invoices and US-based engineering payroll.

chunks.title: The Global Engineering Workforce
chunks.bodyHtml:
Skarion Engineering operates a hybrid model. We have US-based Project Managers (W2 Employees) and highly skilled offshore CAD/GIS draftsmen (1099 Contractors/Vendors).

This means our cash outfalls fall into two massive buckets: Accounts Payable (paying the offshore design firms via wire transfer) and Payroll (paying our US engineers via direct deposit).

hotspots.label: Invoice Number
hotspots.info: Crucial! Always enter the exact invoice number to avoid duplicate wires.

hotspots.label: Project Code
hotspots.info: We must tag this expense to the specific fiber build (e.g., Project Alpha 500-mile build) to track project profitability.

hotspots.label: Total USD Amount
hotspots.info: Make sure the conversion rate is correct if billed in foreign currency. This hits AP.

chunks.title: Payroll Liabilities
chunks.bodyHtml:
For our US-based engineers, the gross wage isn't what they receive. You must withhold taxes and record employer taxes.

For a $10,000 gross payroll run for the US team, the entry looks like this:

  • Debit Engineering Wages Expense: $10,000
  • Credit Payroll Liabilities (Taxes withheld): $2,000
  • Credit Cash (Net Pay): $8,000

chunks.title: Record an Offshore Drafting Bill
chunks.scenarioHtml: We received a $4,500 invoice from Global CAD Solutions for drafting 100 miles of LLD. We will pay it in 30 days. Record the receipt of the bill.
